title: viola arvensis var. media
intro: Viola arvensis var. media, also known as the pale field pansy, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the viola genus. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditional herbal preparations often involve infusions of the plant, as well as the use of its leaves and flowers in teas, tinctures, and salves, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness.
